name: GeoRSCT
description: A geospatial regression benchmark for evaluating representation-solver compatibility. Contains 31,789 U.S. ZIP Code Tabulation Areas (ZCTAs) across the contiguous United States (lower 48 + DC) with 63 solver-usable features (33 ACS + 14 spatial lags + 16 enrichment) and 27 regression targets spanning health, socioeconomic, and environmental domains. Includes three spatially-blocked evaluation protocols (imputation, extrapolation, super-resolution) with fixed splits stratified by SVI quartile, urban/rural density, and hospital access. Published schema: GeoRSCT v23.0.2.
citeAs: Rudolph A. Martin. GeoRSCT: A Geospatial Regression Benchmark for Representation-Solver Compatibility. Version 23.0.2, Hugging Face Datasets, 2026. Theory reference: Intelligence as Representation-Solver Compatibility, Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.18854651; SSRN: 6339299.

distribution.name: georsct_simplified_001.geoparquet
distribution.description: Primary GeoParquet table: 31,789 ZCTAs x 105 columns (7 metadata + 3 splits + 4 coverage + 33 ACS + 14 spatial lags + 16 enrichment + 27 targets + 1 geometry). Single row per ZCTA. CRS: EPSG:4326.

distribution.name: georsct_table.parquet
distribution.description: Same as main GeoParquet but without geometry column. Lightweight for tabular-only workflows.

distribution.name: cdc_places_ci.parquet
distribution.description: Companion sidecar: CDC PLACES 95% confidence intervals for 21 health targets (42 columns: *_ci_low + *_ci_high). Joins on zcta_id.

distribution.name: zcta_acs_margins_of_error.parquet
distribution.description: Companion sidecar: ACS margins of error for 31 features. Joins on zcta_id.

distribution.name: georsct_schema.json
distribution.description: Column metadata, types, and summary statistics.

distribution.name: croissant.json
distribution.description: Machine-readable Croissant metadata for GeoRSCT, including core dataset metadata, source provenance, and Responsible AI metadata for NeurIPS Evaluations & Datasets review.

recordSet.name: georsct-main
recordSet.description: Primary benchmark table: 31,789 ZCTAs (lower 48 + DC), 104 analytic columns plus geometry in the GeoParquet. One row per ZCTA. 63 features usable by solvers (33 ACS + 14 spatial lags + 16 enrichment).

field.name: split_imputation
field.description: County-blocked 5-fold CV split (valid1-valid5, test). Tests geographic interpolation.

field.name: split_extrapolation
field.description: State-blocked 4-fold CV split (valid1-valid4, test). Tests distribution shift across unseen states.

field.name: split_superres
field.description: County-to-ZCTA super-resolution split (train, test). 20% of counties held out.

field.name: has_home_value
field.description: ACS home value data available (98.3% coverage)

field.name: acs_pct_walk_bike
field.description: % walk or bike to work, ACS 2022 5-Year

field.name: acs_gini_index
field.description: Gini index of income inequality, ACS 2022 5-Year

field.name: lag_acs_total_pop
field.description: Neighborhood population density context (Queen-contiguity weighted neighbor mean)

field.name: lag_acs_gini_index
field.description: Neighborhood inequality (SVI uses absolute, not relative)

field.name: svi_socioeconomic
field.description: SVI Theme 1: Socioeconomic status percentile [0-1], CDC/ATSDR 2022

field.name: svi_minority_language
field.description: SVI Theme 3: Racial & ethnic minority percentile [0-1]

field.name: flood_pct_zone_a
field.description: % ZCTA area in 100-year flood zone (SFHA) [0-100], FEMA NFHL polygon overlay in EPSG:5070

field.name: flood_pct_zone_x
field.description: % ZCTA area in minimal risk zone [0-100]

field.name: hifld_n_hospitals
field.description: Number of hospitals in ZCTA, derived from HIFLD Open Data.

field.name: hifld_n_hospital_beds
field.description: Total hospital beds in ZCTA, derived from HIFLD Open Data.

field.name: hifld_nearest_hospital_km
field.description: Haversine distance to nearest hospital (km), derived from HIFLD Open Data.

field.name: hifld_n_pharmacies
field.description: Number of pharmacies in ZCTA, derived from CDC Vaccines.gov / pharmacy access source data.

field.name: hifld_nearest_pharmacy_km
field.description: Haversine distance to nearest pharmacy (km), derived from CDC Vaccines.gov / pharmacy access source data.

field.name: hifld_nearest_trauma_center_km
field.description: Haversine distance to nearest trauma center (km), derived from HIFLD Open Data.

field.name: drive_min_to_nearest_hospital
field.description: OSRM/OpenStreetMap road-network drive time to nearest hospital (minutes).

field.name: drive_min_to_county_seat
field.description: OSRM/OpenStreetMap road-network drive time to county seat (minutes).

field.name: target_annual_checkup
field.description: % adults with annual checkup, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_bp_medicated
field.description: % with BP on medication, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_dental_visit
field.description: % adults with dental visit, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_high_blood_pressure
field.description: % adults with high BP, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_high_cholesterol
field.description: % adults with high cholesterol, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_mental_health_not_good
field.description: % adults poor mental health days, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_night_lights
field.description: Mean nighttime radiance (log10 nW/cm2/sr), VIIRS NASA/NOAA

field.name: target_physical_health_not_good
field.description: % adults poor physical health days, CDC PLACES 2023

field.name: target_tree_cover
field.description: % tree canopy cover, Hansen Global Forest Change

field.name: geometry
field.description: Simplified ZCTA polygon geometry, CRS EPSG:4326. Included in the GeoParquet file.

recordSet.name: cdc-places-ci
recordSet.description: CDC PLACES 95% confidence intervals for the 21 health targets. Joins to georsct-main on zcta_id.

field.name: zcta_id
field.description: 5-digit ZCTA identifier used to join to georsct-main.

recordSet.name: acs-moe
recordSet.description: ACS margins of error for 31 ACS features. Joins to georsct-main on zcta_id.

field.name: zcta_id
field.description: 5-digit ZCTA identifier used to join to georsct-main.

rai:dataLimitations: GeoRSCT is a ZCTA-level benchmark for evaluating geospatial regression and representation-solver compatibility, not an individual-level dataset. Its applicability is limited to aggregate, area-level analysis over the contiguous United States and Washington, DC. Results may not generalize to Alaska, Hawaii, U.S. territories, non-U.S. geographies, finer geographies such as blocks or households, or future time periods without revalidation. ZCTAs are statistical approximations of postal delivery areas and are not stable administrative units; conclusions are subject to aggregation effects and the modifiable areal unit problem. Some targets are modeled estimates rather than direct measurements, and upstream coverage, confidence intervals, survey margins of error, missingness, spatial autocorrelation, and CRS/overlay choices can affect downstream evaluation. The dataset is not recommended for individual risk prediction, clinical decision-making, eligibility determination, enforcement, insurance pricing, credit allocation, or direct resource allocation without additional domain validation, uncertainty analysis, and local stakeholder review.
rai:dataBiases: Known and suspected biases include survey non-response and sampling bias in ACS inputs; BRFSS/CDC PLACES modeling and coverage bias in small-area health estimates; demographic and socioeconomic skew across ZCTAs; rural/urban imbalance; spatial clustering that can inflate naive cross-validation estimates; uncertainty in facility-location and service-access sources; OpenStreetMap/OSRM road-network completeness and routing-profile bias; remote-sensing measurement bias for VIIRS night lights, elevation, flood overlays, and tree cover; and differential missingness in low-population or geographically unusual areas. These biases may cause models to perform differently across population density, income, race/ethnicity composition, language, age structure, transportation access, health-access, and vulnerability regimes.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The dataset does not contain individual-level records, names, street addresses, direct personal identifiers, household-level records, or protected health information. It does contain geographically indexed aggregate attributes related to socioeconomic status, age, race/ethnicity, language, housing, transportation, health prevalence estimates, vulnerability, facility access, and environmental exposure at the ZCTA level. These aggregate variables can still be sensitive when used to profile communities, target interventions, make eligibility decisions, or rank places.
rai:dataUseCases: Validated use cases include geospatial regression benchmarking, evaluation of spatially blocked generalization, analysis of representation-solver compatibility, N-ceiling/irreducible-difficulty estimation, diagnostic model evaluation, reproducibility studies, and teaching or research on geospatial machine-learning failure modes. The dataset is designed to measure how different solver families and representation choices behave under geographic interpolation, extrapolation, and super-resolution-style protocols. Validity has not been established for individual-level inference, causal claims, policy impact estimation, operational triage, medical or emergency service decisions, legal/compliance determinations, or direct claims about the quality of specific communities.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Positive impacts include more transparent geospatial AI evaluation, clearer separation of task difficulty from model skill, improved documentation of spatial leakage and aggregation risks, and better diagnostic tools for benchmark users. Potential negative impacts include stigmatizing places or demographic groups, over-trusting area-level estimates, using aggregate health and socioeconomic variables for profiling, or deploying models in resource-allocation contexts without appropriate uncertainty and fairness review. Mitigations include aggregate-only release, explicit spatially blocked splits, uncertainty sidecars for ACS and CDC PLACES, source-provenance metadata, known-limitations documentation, and warnings against individual-level or high-stakes operational use without additional validation.
usageInfo: GeoRSCT is released under CC-BY-4.0. Users should preserve attribution to GeoRSCT and upstream data sources. OpenStreetMap-derived routing features require OpenStreetMap attribution and attention to ODbL obligations. Hansen Global Forest Change-derived tree-cover features require CC-BY-4.0 attribution. U.S. government data sources are generally public domain, but source attribution is retained for reproducibility.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: American Community Survey (ACS) 2022 5-Year
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: ZCTA-level demographic, socioeconomic, housing, commuting, income, home value, and margins-of-error inputs.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: CDC PLACES 2023 ZCTA data
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: Small-area health outcome targets and confidence-interval sidecar.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: CDC/ATSDR Social Vulnerability Index (SVI)
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: Social vulnerability enrichment features and stratification signals.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: HIFLD Open Data
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: Hospital, hospital-bed, trauma-center, and infrastructure access features.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: CDC Vaccines.gov / pharmacy access source data
OpenStreetMap data via OSRM routing
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: Road-network routing inputs used to compute drive-time features.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: Open Source Routing Machine (OSRM)
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: Routing engine used with OpenStreetMap road-network data.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: U.S. Census TIGER/Line / ZCTA geometry
prov:wasDerivedFrom.role: ZCTA geometries, spatial joins, overlays, and blocked split construction.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: USGS National Elevation Dataset / The National Map elevation data

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Data collection and source harmonization
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Collected publicly available source data for contiguous U.S. ZCTAs and Washington, DC, including ACS, CDC PLACES, CDC/ATSDR SVI, FEMA NFHL, HIFLD Open Data, CDC Vaccines.gov/pharmacy access data, OpenStreetMap/OSRM routing, Census TIGER/Line geometry, VIIRS nighttime lights, USGS elevation data, and Hansen Global Forest Change.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Spatial preprocessing and joins
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Aligned geospatial layers to ZCTA units, performed area overlays and nearest-facility computations, computed road-network drive-time features, retained centroid latitude/longitude, and stored simplified ZCTA polygon geometry in EPSG:4326.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Feature and target construction
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Constructed 63 solver-usable features from ACS, selected spatial lags, and enrichment sources, and constructed 27 regression targets spanning health, socioeconomic, and environmental domains.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Evaluation split construction
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Generated three fixed spatially blocked evaluation protocols: county-blocked imputation, state-blocked extrapolation, and county-to-ZCTA super-resolution. Splits were stratified by social vulnerability, urban/rural density, and hospital access.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Uncertainty and validation sidecars
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Released CDC PLACES confidence interval and ACS margins-of-error sidecars keyed by zcta_id to support uncertainty-aware evaluation and documented schema, lineage, build metadata, and known limitations in accompanying files.
